title: Implement authentication and authorization middleware across all backend APIs
description: Implement robust authentication and authorization middleware for all backend REST and gRPC APIs, including the Equipment Catalog, Finance, Customer Vetting, Social Media Engine, RMS, and Morgan web chat widget. This includes JWT or API key validation for all non-public endpoints, service-to-service authentication (mTLS or shared JWT), RBAC checks for sensitive/admin endpoints, and session/rate limiting and abuse prevention for the web chat widget. Decision points dp-7 and dp-14 must be resolved before implementation. Ensure all endpoints are protected according to their sensitivity and intended audience.
subtasks: Select and document the authentication mechanism (JWT, mTLS, API key) for each service and endpoint type.
Implement authentication middleware in each backend service (Rust/Axum, Go/gRPC, Node.js/Elysia) to validate JWTs or API keys on all non-public endpoints.
Implement service-to-service authentication for internal API calls (e.g., mTLS or shared JWT).
Implement RBAC checks for admin/sensitive endpoints (e.g., payroll, vetting, finance reports) based on user roles or attributes.
Integrate session token generation and validation for the Morgan web chat widget, with expiry and per-session/IP rate limiting.
Add input length limits and content filtering on chat messages to prevent prompt injection and abuse.
Implement abuse detection heuristics (e.g., rapid-fire messages, known injection patterns) and optional CAPTCHA for chat session initiation.
Update OpenAPI/gRPC documentation to reflect authentication requirements.
Run integration and security tests to verify all endpoints enforce authentication and authorization as intended.
acceptance_criteria: All non-public API endpoints require valid authentication tokens or mTLS.
Admin/sensitive endpoints enforce RBAC or equivalent access control.
Morgan web chat widget enforces session authentication, rate limiting, and input validation.
Abuse detection and prompt injection defenses are active on chat endpoints.
All authentication failures are logged as security events.
Automated tests confirm unauthorized requests are rejected with appropriate error codes.
OpenAPI/gRPC documentation accurately describes authentication requirements.

title: Enforce authorization boundaries and audit logging for Morgan AI agent MCP tool invocations
description: Implement strict authorization boundaries, rate limiting, and audit logging for all MCP tool invocations by the Morgan AI agent. Define which tools require human-in-the-loop approval, enforce per-tool rate limits, implement amount/scope limits for financial operations, and ensure all tool invocations are logged with caller context. Prevent the agent from escalating its own permissions.
subtasks: Define and document permission boundaries for each MCP tool (e.g., which require human approval, which are self-serve).
Implement per-tool rate limiting and scope/amount limits (e.g., max invoice amount without approval).
Add audit logging for every tool invocation, including caller identity, tool name, parameters, and outcome.
Implement checks to prevent the agent from escalating its own permissions or bypassing approval workflows.
Test all tool invocations for correct enforcement of authorization, rate limits, and audit logging.
Review and update documentation for tool invocation security.
acceptance_criteria: All MCP tool invocations are subject to defined authorization boundaries and rate limits.
Sensitive tools (e.g., invoice creation, social publishing) require human approval where specified.
All tool invocations are logged with full context and are auditable.
Attempts to exceed limits or bypass approval are blocked and logged.
Automated tests confirm enforcement of all boundaries and logging.
No privilege escalation path exists for the agent.

title: Implement encryption at rest and in transit for all financial and PII data
description: Ensure all financial data (invoices, payments, payroll) and customer PII are encrypted at rest and in transit. Enable PostgreSQL storage encryption, implement column-level encryption for sensitive fields, enforce TLS on all database connections, and ensure database backups are encrypted. Classify all PII, implement access logging, data retention, and right-to-erasure capabilities for compliance.
subtasks: Enable storage-level encryption for PostgreSQL using encrypted storage classes or volume encryption.
Implement column-level encryption (e.g., pgcrypto) for highly sensitive fields (e.g., payment intent IDs, payroll amounts, PII).
Configure all PostgreSQL and Redis connection strings to require TLS (sslmode=require or verify-full).
Verify from application pods that all database connections are encrypted (e.g., check pg_stat_ssl).
Ensure all database backups are encrypted at rest (e.g., S3/R2 server-side encryption).
Classify all data fields by sensitivity (PII, financial, public) in the schema and documentation.
Implement access logging for all PII read operations.
Define and enforce data retention policies for PII and vetting results.
Implement data anonymization/deletion endpoints or scripts for GDPR/CCPA compliance.
Review and update documentation for data protection and privacy compliance.
Test encryption at rest, in transit, and right-to-erasure workflows.
acceptance_criteria: All financial and PII data is encrypted at rest and in transit.
Sensitive fields are encrypted at the column level where required.
All database connections from applications enforce TLS.
Database backups are encrypted at rest.
Access to PII is logged and auditable.
Data retention and deletion policies are implemented and testable.
Automated tests confirm encryption and compliance controls are effective.

title: Harden Stripe webhook endpoint with strict signature verification and replay protection
description: Ensure the Stripe webhook handler strictly verifies the Stripe-Signature header against the raw request body using a timing-safe comparison, implements replay protection, and rejects invalid or expired webhooks. Add comprehensive test cases for signature forgery, replay, and timestamp expiration.
subtasks: Update the Stripe webhook handler to verify signatures using the raw request body and Stripe's official library.
Implement timing-safe comparison for signature validation.
Add replay protection by checking webhook event timestamps (default tolerance 300 seconds).
Reject webhooks with invalid or missing signatures with a 400 status.
Add test cases for forged signatures, replayed events, and expired timestamps.
Document the webhook security strategy and update runbooks.
acceptance_criteria: Webhook handler rejects all requests with invalid or missing signatures.
Replay and expired events are detected and rejected.
All signature verification uses timing-safe comparison.
Automated tests cover forgery, replay, and expiration scenarios.
No payment status can be forged via webhook manipulation.

title: Enforce parameterized queries and SQL injection prevention across all services
description: Mandate the use of parameterized queries in all database access code across all services (Rust/sqlx, Go/pgx, Node.js/pg). Add code review checklist items, compile-time query verification, and automated SQL injection testing for endpoints that accept user input.
subtasks: Audit all database access code to ensure parameterized queries are used exclusively (no string interpolation).
Add compile-time query verification (e.g., sqlx::query! macro) where supported.
Update code review checklists to include SQL injection prevention.
Add automated tests and fuzzing for endpoints that accept user input (e.g., search, barcode scan).
Run SQL injection test cases against all endpoints that interact with the database.
Document the SQL injection prevention strategy.
acceptance_criteria: All database queries use parameterized statements.
No string interpolation is present in SQL queries.
Automated tests and code reviews catch any violations.
SQL injection test cases do not succeed against any endpoint.
Compile-time query verification is enabled where possible.

title: Harden file upload endpoint with malware scanning, EXIF stripping, re-encoding, and rate limiting
description: Enhance the Social Media Engine's file upload endpoint with comprehensive security controls: strip EXIF/metadata, re-encode images, validate dimensions, apply rate limiting, and integrate malware scanning if feasible.
subtasks: Integrate EXIF/metadata stripping for all uploaded images before S3 upload.
Re-encode images using a known-safe image library to prevent polyglot attacks.
Validate image dimensions and reject files with abnormally large dimensions.
Apply rate limiting specifically to the upload endpoint.
Integrate virus/malware scanning for uploaded files if feasible (e.g., ClamAV).
Add automated tests for all upload security controls.
acceptance_criteria: All uploaded images are stripped of EXIF/metadata before storage.
Images are re-encoded and validated for safe formats and dimensions.
Upload endpoint enforces rate limits per user/IP.
Malware scanning is performed on all uploads or documented as infeasible.
Automated tests verify all controls are enforced.

title: Accelerate external secret management and bootstrap phase hardening
description: Deploy the External Secrets Operator (ESO) as early as possible in the infrastructure pipeline to minimize the window where secrets exist as plain Kubernetes objects. Enable etcd encryption at rest for Kubernetes Secrets and consider Sealed Secrets for the bootstrap phase.
subtasks: Move ESO deployment to the earliest possible infrastructure provisioning step.
Ensure no secret values (even placeholders) are committed to source control.
Enable etcd encryption at rest for Kubernetes Secrets.
Implement Sealed Secrets or equivalent for the bootstrap phase before ESO is operational.
Audit all manifests and CI pipelines for accidental secret exposure.
Test secret rotation and access controls in the new workflow.
acceptance_criteria: ESO is deployed before any application secrets are created.
No secrets are present in source control or unencrypted in the cluster.
etcd encryption at rest is enabled and verified.
Bootstrap secrets are protected via Sealed Secrets or equivalent.
No window exists where secrets are unprotected in production.

title: Implement container image security scanning, signing, and trusted registry enforcement
description: Add CI pipeline steps to scan all container images for vulnerabilities, pin base images to digests, use minimal/distroless images, enforce trusted registry policies, and sign custom images with cosign. Enforce these policies at cluster admission.
subtasks: Integrate container image scanning (e.g., Trivy, Grype) into CI pipelines for all services.
Pin all base images to specific digest hashes in Dockerfiles.
Switch to distroless or minimal base images for all custom services.
Deploy an admission controller (e.g., Kyverno, OPA Gatekeeper) to block images from untrusted registries.
Sign all custom images with cosign and verify signatures at admission.
Document the image security policy and update developer onboarding.
acceptance_criteria: All images are scanned for vulnerabilities before deployment.
No images are pulled from untrusted registries.
All base images are pinned to digests.
Admission controller blocks non-compliant images.
All custom images are signed and verified at admission.

title: Enforce dependency pinning and automated supply chain security auditing
description: Ensure all projects use lockfiles for dependency pinning, add CI steps for automated vulnerability auditing (cargo audit, npm audit), configure Dependabot or Renovate for updates, and perform an initial dependency audit before deployment.
subtasks: Commit lockfiles (Cargo.lock, package-lock.json, bun.lockb) for all projects to source control.
Add CI steps to run cargo audit and npm audit on every build.
Configure Dependabot or Renovate for automated dependency update PRs.
Pin transitive dependencies where possible.
Perform an initial dependency audit and remediate any critical findings.
Document the supply chain security process.
acceptance_criteria: All projects have lockfiles committed and enforced.
CI fails on critical dependency vulnerabilities.
Automated update PRs are generated for outdated dependencies.
Initial audit is completed and all critical issues are resolved.

title: Implement centralized application-layer security event logging and SIEM integration
description: Define a structured security event log format and implement security event emission in all services for authentication failures, authorization denials, suspicious API patterns, rate limit triggers, and webhook verification failures. Integrate with the centralized logging stack and create alerting rules for security event patterns.
subtasks: Define a shared security event log schema (event_type, severity, source_ip, user/session, resource, action, outcome).
Implement security event emission in all services for relevant events (auth failures, authz denials, rate limit triggers, input validation failures, webhook failures).
Ship security events to the centralized logging stack with appropriate retention.
Create alerting rules for suspicious security event patterns (e.g., >10 auth failures from same IP in 5 minutes).
Test end-to-end event logging and alerting for all relevant scenarios.
Document the security event logging and monitoring process.
acceptance_criteria: All relevant security events are logged in a structured format.
Events are shipped to the centralized logging stack and retained as required.
Alerting rules are in place and trigger on suspicious patterns.
Automated tests confirm event emission and alerting for all scenarios.
